name: systematic-debugging
summary: Find root cause before proposing or implementing a bug fix.
use_when: A bug, failing test, regression, or unexpected behavior needs root-cause diagnosis before a fix.

name: no-fluff
summary: Compress responses while keeping technical content intact.
use_when: The user asks for concise, compressed, focused, or no-fluff communication.

name: writing-plans
summary: Turn a spec into an explicit implementation plan with exact paths and checks.
use_when: A multi-step implementation or a conceptual design/redesign needs an explicit plan before code changes.

name: ai-slop-cleaner
summary: Clean up AI-generated code with tests-first, smell-by-smell discipline.
use_when: Use when asked to clean up, refactor, or 'deslop' AI-generated code that is bloated, noisy, or over-abstracted.
avoid_when: Do not use for implementing new features or making broad architectural rewrites without tests.

name: requesting-code-review
summary: Request a focused review using plan context and an explicit change scope.

name: receiving-code-review
summary: Evaluate review feedback before implementing it.

name: pr-triage
summary: Rank pull requests by urgency, impact, and merge risk.
use_when: Use when open pull requests, stale branches, or review bottlenecks need prioritization from the current repository state.
avoid_when: Do not use for deep code review of a single PR or CI debugging.

name: repo-map
summary: Map an unfamiliar codebase before making changes.

name: bug-hunt
summary: Reproduce, isolate, and fix a bug with minimal scope.
use_when: Reproduce, isolate, and fix a bug with the smallest safe change.

name: safe-refactor
summary: Restructure code in small steps without changing behavior.
use_when: Code needs restructuring while preserving behavior, with tests as the safety net.

name: defuddle
summary: Extract clean Markdown from web pages using Defuddle CLI.
use_when: A web page or HTML source should be extracted into clean Markdown context.

name: markitdown-file-intake
summary: Convert supported documents into Markdown context using Microsoft MarkItDown.
use_when: A document file should be converted into Markdown for agent-readable context.

name: ask-claude
summary: Ask Claude through the local Claude CLI and save a reusable artifact.
use_when: Use when a local agent needs a focused second opinion from the Claude CLI without MCP routing.
avoid_when: Do not use when Claude CLI is missing or for tasks that require interactive back-and-forth.

name: ask-gemini
summary: Ask Gemini through the local Gemini CLI and save a reusable artifact.
use_when: Use when a local agent needs a focused second opinion or brainstorming from the Gemini CLI without MCP routing.
avoid_when: Do not use for multi-turn advisory sessions where context must persist across follow-ups; use relay-gemini instead.

name: ask-codex
summary: Ask Codex through the local Codex CLI and save a reusable artifact.
use_when: Use when an agent needs to delegate a focused question, subtask, or review to a local Codex instance.
avoid_when: Do not use for tasks requiring interactive back-and-forth; output is captured once.

name: using-powerups
summary: Discover, select, check, and apply installed Agent Powerups safely.
use_when: A task may benefit from installed Agent Powerups and needs task-based discovery before choosing a workflow.

name: bigquery-cost-audit
summary: Audit BigQuery costs with INFORMATION_SCHEMA query templates for hotspot detection, failure analysis, and partition or clustering optimization.

name: data-quality
summary: Reusable dbt data quality patterns for warehouse-backed analytics projects.

name: dbt-incremental-strategy-audit
summary: Audit whether a dbt incremental model uses the right strategy for the repo, data shape, and operations.

name: dbt-preflight
summary: Inspect changed dbt assets, estimate blast radius, and recommend the narrowest safe validation plan.

name: dbt-strategy
summary: Reusable dbt modeling strategy for warehouse-backed analytics projects.

name: metric-impact-analyzer
summary: Classify metric and semantic model changes by risk level and identify downstream BI breakage, required validation, and stakeholder sign-off.

name: sql-business-logic-review
summary: Review SQL for business logic correctness, aggregation risk, and silent definition changes.

name: github-local
summary: Verified local GitHub MCP check, smoke, and install flow for Codex, Claude Code, and generic clients.

mcp.warning: Requires Docker. Uses ghcr.io/github/github-mcp-server (official). Set GITHUB_TOKEN or GITHUB_PAT env var before use. The ghcr.io image is currently unpinned (latest) by default; it is strongly recommended to pin to a specific digest in production.

name: typescript-app
summary: Starter AGENTS.md template for a TypeScript application repository.

name: python-library
summary: Starter AGENTS.md template for a Python library repository.

name: dbt-project
summary: Starter AGENTS.md template for a dbt project.

name: ml-project
summary: Starter AGENTS.md template for a machine learning project.

name: open-source-maintainer
summary: Starter AGENTS.md template for open source maintainer workflows.

name: ship-check
summary: Manual pre-handoff command checklist for validating and reporting changes.
use_when: Use to prepare a change for handoff manually by inspecting diffs, running tests, and validating assets.
avoid_when: Do not use to actually commit, push, open PRs, or edit config.

name: using-powerups-command
summary: Manual command prompt for discovering and applying installed Agent Powerups.
use_when: Use when starting non-trivial work in a repo to discover and apply installed Agent Powerups.
avoid_when: Do not use if the user already specified exactly which powerup or tool to use.

name: no-secrets-preflight
summary: Review-before-use hook recipe for blocking obvious secret leaks.

name: handoff-summary
summary: Review-before-use hook recipe for complete final handoff summaries.

name: validation-required
summary: Review-before-use hook recipe for blocking unverified completion claims.

name: minimal-setup-example
summary: Minimal disposable-root setup example for reviewing Agent Powerups safely.

name: codex-setup-example
summary: Codex setup example using dry-run and explicit agent root staging.

name: claude-code-setup-example
summary: Claude Code setup example using dry-run and explicit agent root staging.

name: feature-iteration
summary: Small test-first workflow for shipping one asset or CLI behavior at a time.
use_when: Use to iterate on adding a single Agent Powerups asset or CLI feature using a small, testable loop.
avoid_when: Do not use for massive rewrites or broad compatibility changes.

name: agent-relay
summary: Persistent local Gemini ACP relay for delegating repeat questions, reviews, and research turns to an always-active secondary agent.
use_when: Use to keep a secondary agent process active (a local persistent relay) for delegated multi-turn tasks.
avoid_when: Do not use for single-shot questions where ask-gemini, ask-claude, or ask-codex are faster.

name: validate-skills
summary: Validate that every skill folder has a non-empty SKILL.md with required frontmatter.

name: validate-catalog
summary: Validate catalog structure, allowed values, and shipped asset coverage.

name: validate-mirrors
summary: Validate that plugin skill copies stay byte-identical with their root skills, with an explicit allowlist for intentional variants.

name: check-requirements
summary: Report availability of external commands and packages declared in catalog requirements.

name: relay-gemini
summary: Relay questions and tasks to a persistent local Gemini ACP session with cross-turn context.
use_when: Use when a persistent local Gemini ACP session should keep context across multiple advisory turns.
avoid_when: Do not use for single focused questions with no follow-up; use ask-gemini instead.

name: relay-claude
summary: Relay questions and tasks to a local Claude CLI using one-shot subprocesses per ask.
use_when: Use when another local agent needs a one-shot Claude CLI answer without cross-turn relay memory.
avoid_when: Do not use for multi-turn advisory context; use relay-gemini or a dedicated session workflow.

name: relay-codex
summary: Relay questions and tasks to a local Codex CLI using one-shot subprocesses per ask.
use_when: Use when another local agent needs a one-shot Codex CLI answer and interactive behavior is acceptable.
avoid_when: Do not use when an interactive TUI would block automation.

name: graphify
summary: Use upstream graphify and the official graphifyy package to build and query knowledge graphs from local corpora.

name: brainstorming
summary: Force design-first exploration and user approval before any implementation begins.
use_when: Before any creative work — designing a new feature, component, or behavior — the intent and design space should be explored first.

name: dispatching-parallel-agents
summary: Orchestrate two or more independent tasks in parallel subagents with structured output collection.
use_when: Use when facing multiple independent tasks (e.g. unrelated failures) that can be worked on without shared state or sequential dependencies.
avoid_when: Do not use for dependent tasks that must be resolved in order.

name: test-driven-development
summary: Enforce REDâ†’GREENâ†’REFACTOR discipline with an Iron Law that prevents test-after or skip patterns.
use_when: A new behavior or bugfix should be driven by a failing test before implementation.

name: verification-before-completion
summary: Gate work completion behind explicit requirement verification before claiming done.
use_when: Before claiming work is complete, requirements and validation evidence must be checked explicitly.

name: writing-skills
summary: Create well-formed SKILL.md files with correct structure, trigger clauses, and Claude Search Optimization.
use_when: Use when creating new skills, editing existing skills, or verifying skills work before deployment using Test-Driven Development.
avoid_when: Do not use when simply reading or applying an existing skill.

name: finishing-a-development-branch
summary: Present a four-option merge/PR/keep/discard menu and execute the chosen path cleanly.
use_when: Use when implementation is complete, all tests pass, and you need to decide how to integrate the work (merge, PR, or cleanup).
avoid_when: Do not use if tests are still failing or implementation is incomplete.

name: using-git-worktrees
summary: Set up and work inside isolated git worktrees to avoid cross-branch contamination.
use_when: Use when starting feature work that needs isolated workspaces sharing the same repository without switching branches.
avoid_when: Do not use for simple single-file edits that do not require checkout isolation.

name: json-canvas
summary: Create and validate JSON Canvas files for node-based visual layouts.
use_when: Use when working with .canvas files to create visual canvases, mind maps, or flowcharts, especially for Obsidian.
avoid_when: Do not use for drawing HTML canvases or generic image generation.

name: gh-address-comments
summary: Read GitHub PR review threads with GraphQL and address each comment with targeted fixes.
use_when: Use when addressing actionable GitHub pull request review feedback, inspecting review threads, or implementing fixes.
avoid_when: Do not use for creating a new PR or when review comments are not actionable.

name: dependency-review
summary: Review-before-use hook recipe for auditing new packages before they land in dependency manifests.

name: test-gate
summary: Review-before-use hook recipe for blocking completion and commits when tests are red.

name: lint-check
summary: Review-before-use hook recipe for enforcing linter and formatter checks before commits.

name: session-context-restore
summary: Review-before-use hook recipe for surfacing prior session context at session start.

name: auto-commit-message
summary: Review-before-use hook recipe for generating Conventional Commits messages from staged diffs.

name: plan
summary: Structure a task before starting: read request, map files, write step list, confirm scope.
use_when: Use to structure a task by mapping the affected area, reading current state, and writing atomic steps before starting.
avoid_when: Do not use to actually write or modify code directly without user approval of the plan.

name: review
summary: Code review workflow: read diff, check correctness, check tests, check style, write findings.
use_when: Use when the user wants a systematic code review of a diff or branch, checking correctness, tests, and style.
avoid_when: Do not use for executing tests or applying fixes directly.

name: debug
summary: Debugging session: reproduce, isolate, hypothesize, verify, fix, confirm.
use_when: Use to systematically reproduce, isolate, and fix a bug using a reproduce-isolate-fix loop rather than speculative patching.
avoid_when: Do not use for adding new features or broad refactoring.

name: implement
summary: Feature implementation: read spec, write failing test, implement, validate, update docs.
use_when: Use to guide test-first feature implementation that stays strictly scoped to an agreed specification.
avoid_when: Do not use for exploratory coding or before ambiguities in the spec have been clarified.

name: triage
summary: Issue and PR triage: list open items, categorize by impact and urgency, assign priorities.
use_when: Use to turn a backlog (issues, tasks, PRs) into a clear, ranked action list grouped by type and scored by impact/effort.
avoid_when: Do not use to actually close, assign, or modify the issues yourself.

name: search-before-building
summary: Check existing repo capability, external libraries, MCP options, and maintenance risk before writing custom code. Decide adopt/wrap/build with explicit criteria.

name: context-retrieval-loop
summary: Deterministic 3-cycle loop for gathering codebase context before acting. Broad rg, exact source and tests, target-specific docs. Stop after enough context or report gaps.
use_when: Use when starting work in an unfamiliar area of a codebase or spawning a subagent that needs targeted file context.
avoid_when: Do not use if the exact files needed are already known and verified.

name: agent-harness-design
summary: Design agent tool sets with stable names, narrow schemas, deterministic output shapes, and explicit error paths. No catch-all tools unless unavoidable.
use_when: Use when designing tool definitions for an agent, observing high retry rates/ambiguous invocations, or reviewing an existing harness.
avoid_when: Do not use for designing standard application features; this is strictly for agent tooling.

name: agent-config-security-audit
summary: Audit agent config files for security vulnerabilities and misconfigurations. Covers settings.json, .mcp.json, .codex/config.toml, AGENTS.md, hooks, and plugin manifests. Classifies P0/P1 findings.
use_when: Agent configuration files (settings.json, .mcp.json, config.toml, hooks) need a security audit.

name: hook-safety-review
summary: Review a hook definition before enabling it: trigger surface, command injection, outbound network, silent failure, and scope, with P0/P1/Note verdicts.
use_when: Use when a hook is about to be enabled or modified (e.g., pre-commit, agent lifecycle hook, or third-party snippet).
avoid_when: Do not use for broad agent-config audits or reviewing regular application code.

name: mcp-risk-review
summary: Triage an MCP server before enabling it: launch vector pinning, transport exposure, credential handling, tool surface, and scope boundaries, ending in an approval-gated verdict.
use_when: An MCP server is about to be enabled, added, or upgraded.

name: secret-leak-preflight
summary: Scan staged changes, untracked files, and generated artifacts for credentials before committing, pushing, or publishing; on a hit, unstage and recommend rotation.
use_when: Code or config must be scanned for leaked secrets or hardcoded credentials before commit.

name: model-routing
summary: Vendor-neutral routing guide for choosing the right model tier. Mechanical work uses fast; implementation uses standard; architecture, security, and release audit use deep.
use_when: Use when selecting the right model tier (fast/standard/deep) for a task, subagent, or multi-agent pipeline.
avoid_when: Do not use when the model choice is already hardcoded or restricted by the user.

name: requirements-clarifier
summary: Turn vague implementation requests into testable requirements before coding, using one-question-at-a-time clarification and explicit ambiguity reduction.
use_when: A vague or ambiguous implementation request must be turned into testable requirements before coding.

name: hard-won-skill-extractor
summary: Extract a difficult, codebase-specific lesson from a session and turn it into a reusable skill candidate.
use_when: Use when a tricky bug, workaround, or undocumented behavior took real debugging effort and should be captured as a reusable skill.
avoid_when: Do not use for simple copy-paste snippets or things that could be Googled in 5 minutes.

name: persistent-completion-loop
summary: Keep working story-by-story until the task is complete and verified, with a structured PRD and reviewer gate before completion.
use_when: Use when a task must run to verified completion (e.g. user says 'don't stop' or 'keep going until done') across retries.
avoid_when: Do not use for a quick one-shot fix or when the user wants manual control over completion.

name: autonomous-delivery-pipeline
summary: Coordinate spec expansion, planning, implementation, QA, and validation as an opt-in autonomous delivery workflow.
use_when: Use for end-to-end autonomous execution from a brief idea to working, verified code spanning planning, coding, testing, and validation.
avoid_when: Do not use for brainstorming, single focused changes, or quick bug fixes.

name: subagent-team-orchestration
summary: Coordinate multiple subagents through dependency-aware waves, stage handoffs, monitoring, and verification loops.
use_when: Use when a task is too large for one agent and splits cleanly into stages (planning, implementation, review) requiring different specialties.
avoid_when: Do not use when the task fits one focused agent or is purely sequential with no parallel branches.

name: parallel-execution-engine
summary: Run independent work in parallel with explicit wave boundaries, lightweight coordination, and bounded verification.
use_when: Use when latency matters and multiple independent tasks can run at once in concurrent waves without complex stage-based orchestration.
avoid_when: Do not use when dependencies are complex, requiring stage-based orchestration, or when guaranteed persistence across retries is needed.

name: codebase-migration-batches
summary: Run large codebase migrations in reviewable local batches with codemods, checkpoints, and verification.

name: mcp-server-builder
summary: Design workflow-first MCP servers with typed schemas, size-bounded outputs, corrective error messages, and a server-readiness checklist.
use_when: Use when designing or implementing a new MCP server, or refactoring an existing MCP tool surface.
avoid_when: Do not use for merely installing, configuring, or checking an existing MCP server.

name: skill-authoring-guide
summary: Create or refactor skills using a frontmatter template, good/bad description examples, a length guide, and progressive-disclosure structure.
use_when: Use when creating or refactoring a reusable agent skill to ensure high quality, lean frontmatter, and progressive disclosure.
avoid_when: Do not use when simply reading or applying an existing skill.

name: github-ci-failure-triage
summary: Inspect GitHub PR checks, fetch actionable failure logs, summarize the breakage, and propose a local fix plan before changing code.
use_when: Use when a GitHub PR shows failing checks and a clean failure readout/triage is needed before changing any code.
avoid_when: Do not use to actually fix the code directly without presenting the triage readout first.

name: pr-review-ci-loop
summary: Review a PR and triage CI failures in one pass, producing a structured readout of blocking findings, likely fixes, and a validation plan.
use_when: Use when a pull request needs one bounded review and CI triage cycle with explicit approval before edits.
avoid_when: Do not use when the user only asked for a review without CI context or has not approved edits.

name: deploy-pipeline-runbook
summary: Coordinate multi-system deployments as a step-by-step runbook with structured approval checkpoints, explicit rollback plans, and side-effect disclosure.
use_when: Use when a release spans multiple systems or vendors, and the order of operations and rollback planning matter.
avoid_when: Do not use for simple single-system deployments or when explicit human approval is not required.

name: security-audit
summary: Audit agent configuration files for security vulnerabilities and misconfigurations. Classifies findings as P0 or P1.
use_when: Use to audit agent configuration files (e.g., .claude, .mcp.json, AGENTS.md) for security vulnerabilities.
avoid_when: Do not use for general application security scanning or code security review.

name: model-route
summary: Recommend the best model tier for the current task based on complexity, risk, and budget.
use_when: Use to recommend the best model tier (fast, standard, deep) for a specific task based on complexity, risk, and budget.
avoid_when: Do not use when the model is already predetermined or the user explicitly specified the model.

name: quality-gate
summary: Run formatter, linter, and type checks for a file or directory. Report remediation steps.
use_when: Use to run format, lint, and type checks on demand to mirror CI quality gates.
avoid_when: Do not use for deep logic review or fixing complex architectural issues.

name: bug-check
summary: Run automated tests and build checks first, then agent code review. For each bug found, propose or document a regression test.
use_when: Use to mandate automated checks (tests, build) before agent code review, and propose regression tests for bugs.
avoid_when: Do not use to skip automated testing or directly patch bugs speculatively.

name: dev-vitals
summary: Core engineering discipline: task intake, context minimization, and handoffs.

name: debugging-diagnostics
summary: Failure investigation: log forensics, flaky tests, and minimal reproductions.

name: codebase-maintenance
summary: Safe refactoring: dead code removal, naming cleanup, and incremental migrations.

name: data-engineering
summary: Analytics engineering: dbt patterns, BigQuery optimization, and data quality.

name: documentation-systems
summary: Maintainable docs: README hardening, ADRs, and agent-readable context docs.

name: machine-learning-ops
summary: ML hygiene: experiment tracking, leakage checks, and model evaluation.

name: quality-gates
summary: Software quality: change impact check, CI analysis, and release verification.

name: software-engineering
summary: Subagent orchestration, wave execution, persistent completion, autonomous delivery, and migration batching.

name: agentic-systems
summary: Multi-model orchestration and advisor routing.

name: security-guardrails
summary: Security vulnerability detection and review workflows.

name: codebase-intelligence
summary: Search-first codebase understanding, context retrieval, mapping, pattern detection, and living project intelligence.

name: spec-driven-development
summary: Requirements clarification, phase planning, workstreams, and wave-based execution for reviewable delivery.

name: mcp-development
summary: Workflow-first MCP server design with schema-first tools, context-aware outputs, and evaluation discipline.

name: skill-authoring
summary: Skill creation and extraction workflows for reusable agent guidance.

name: github-ops
summary: GitHub review, comment handling, and CI triage workflows with approval-gated write actions.

name: connected-apps
summary: Review-first runbooks for connected-app workflows and multi-system deployment sequencing.

name: browser-automation-safety
summary: Drive browser automation, screenshots, forms, auth sessions, and UI evidence with strict origin, secret, and artifact boundaries.

name: managed-codebase-context
summary: Use managed MCP codebase context sessions safely, with transport checks, freshness checks, timeouts, and process cleanup.
use_when: Use when connecting to a persistent managed codebase-context MCP service to retrieve broad architectural context or symbol inventory.
avoid_when: Do not use to start ephemeral server processes blindly or if standard grep is sufficient.

name: context-compression
summary: Compact bloated conversation context into a structured handoff artifact using a fill-in template, quality probes, and an anti-loss checklist.
use_when: Use when conversation context is bloated, constraints are forgotten, or a compact handoff artifact is needed to resume work.
avoid_when: Do not use at the very beginning of a task before context has accumulated.

name: prompt-evaluation-runner
summary: Build and run minimal prompt/model evals with deterministic assertions, safe env handling, explicit provider cost, and result triage.
use_when: Use when evaluating prompts, LLM outputs, or red-team suites using local eval configurations and cost controls.
avoid_when: Do not use to arbitrarily install unvetted remote runners without checking local toolchains first.

name: agent-runtime-patterns
summary: Optimize agent loops, card packs, MCP session lifecycles, tool-call count, and multi-agent runtime patterns with measurement.
use_when: Use when optimizing slow agent loops, designing multi-agent topologies, or managing MCP session lifecycles.
avoid_when: Do not use for basic single-agent, clear-scope tasks that do not benefit from advanced orchestration.

name: filesystem-mcp-guardrails
summary: Configure and review filesystem MCP access with a path-boundary checklist, method allowlists, and enforced out-of-root request refusal.

name: local-rag-mcp
summary: Query, ingest, maintain, and troubleshoot local RAG MCP corpora for semantic document retrieval while protecting sensitive data.
use_when: Use when querying, ingesting, or maintaining a local RAG MCP corpus for semantic document retrieval.
avoid_when: Do not use for simple grep/glob pattern matching across files.

name: review-comment-style-mining
summary: Mine PR review comments and text diffs for reusable documentation, tone, wording, and editorial improvement patterns.
use_when: Use when mining PR review comments and text diffs to extract reusable writing, documentation, and editorial improvement patterns.
avoid_when: Do not use for deep logic bugs or mechanical code reviews.

name: structured-code-search-mcp
summary: Use MCP-backed structured code search by routing queries to text search, AST query, symbol inventory, or bounded extraction tools in order.
use_when: Use when an agent needs to search, navigate, or extract code using structural queries via an MCP-backed server.
avoid_when: Do not use if standard text search (grep) or regex is sufficient and faster.

name: red-team-eval-authoring
summary: Author red-team eval plugins with structured grader outputs, plugin registration templates, attack prompt fixtures, and explicit harm-class boundaries.
use_when: Use when creating or reviewing red-team eval plugins, attack templates, grader rubrics, or model-risk test metadata.
avoid_when: Do not use to run unvetted networked evals or paid model graders without explicit user approval.

name: agent-session-forensics
summary: Diagnose interrupted agent sessions using jq-based message inspection, tool-call/result correlation, and a failure-pattern triage table.
use_when: Use when diagnosing agent session history, interrupted tool loops, or missing tool results by inspecting session files.
avoid_when: Do not use to actively modify or truncate session histories unless explicitly requested.

name: skill-evaluation-workbench
summary: Design deterministic eval suites with a minimal-case structure, grader-type selection table, and failure-classification guide before editing.
use_when: Use when designing, running, or debugging deterministic eval suites for agent skills, prompts, or MCP-backed cases.
avoid_when: Do not use to blindly execute Docker or remote models without reviewing the runner configuration first.

name: tool-integrations
summary: Browser, MCP, retrieval, and runtime integration skills with conservative setup and safety boundaries.

name: agent-evaluation-lab
summary: Prompt, skill, red-team, and agent behavior evaluation workflows.

name: context7
summary: Fetch up-to-date library and framework documentation via the Context7 MCP server.

mcp.warning: Makes remote HTTP requests to retrieve documentation. Optional: set CONTEXT7_API_KEY for higher rate limits.

name: sequential-thinking
summary: Enable structured, multi-step reasoning chains via the MCP sequential-thinking server.

mcp.warning: Low risk. No filesystem or remote write access. Safe for most environments.

name: playwright
summary: Browser automation via Microsoft Playwright MCP server. Runs headless by default.

mcp.warning: High browser-automation risk. Can visit arbitrary URLs, download files, and interact with web UIs. Runs headless by default; remove --headless for headed mode. Restrict allowed origins in production environments.

name: filesystem-repo-scoped
summary: Filesystem MCP server scoped to the repository root. Requires replacing the ${REPO_ROOT} placeholder before use.

mcp.warning: High local file risk. Grants the agent full read/write access to all files under the scoped path. Replace ${REPO_ROOT} with the absolute path of the repository root. Never scope to the home directory.

name: memory
summary: Persistent local memory via the MCP memory server. Stores and retrieves facts across sessions.

mcp.warning: Persists conversation context to a local JSON file. Do not store secrets, credentials, or personal data in memory. Review the memory file location before use in shared environments.

name: destructive-bash-guard
summary: Review-before-use hook recipe for blocking hard-to-reverse Bash commands like rm -rf and git reset --hard.

name: mcp-write-guard
summary: Review-before-use hook recipe for intercepting MCP write-class tool calls and requiring explicit approval.

name: mcp-config-change-approval
summary: Review-before-use hook recipe for requiring approval before any MCP server configuration file is modified.

name: env-file-mutation-approval
summary: Review-before-use hook recipe for blocking writes to .env files without explicit approval.

name: generated-file-warning
summary: Review-before-use hook recipe for warning when an agent attempts to edit a generated file.

name: console-log-check
summary: Review-before-use hook recipe for catching debug statements in staged changes before a commit.

name: large-diff-warning
summary: Review-before-use hook recipe for warning on oversized commits and prompting to split or confirm.

name: session-log
summary: Review-before-use hook recipe for appending a timestamped audit trail of agent tool calls to a local log file.

name: release-readiness-protocol
summary: Check version bump, changelog, compare range, package metadata, validation commands, publish steps, and rollback plan before cutting a release.

name: changelog-generator
summary: Turn commits, PRs, or diffs into user-facing release notes or an internal changelog, with modes for breaking-change scanning.

name: build-fix-minimal-diff
summary: Fix broken builds, type errors, or test failures with the smallest safe diff â€” no opportunistic refactors, no cleanup.

name: environment-doctor
summary: Diagnose local agent and project setup problems â€” missing commands, broken MCP configs, stale generated assets, bad paths, and missing env vars.
use_when: Use when an agent's commands fail with missing dependencies, MCP servers fail to start, or setup works inconsistently.
avoid_when: Do not use for debugging application logic or test failures that are unrelated to environment setup.

name: webapp-visual-testing
summary: Inspect and test web UIs using screenshots, browser automation, accessibility snapshots, and console/network signals.
use_when: Use when a browser-facing UI needs visual verification, layout bug checking, or an end-to-end browser workflow test.
avoid_when: Do not use for testing standard REST APIs or headless background jobs.

name: agent-readable-docs
summary: Use when writing technical documentation that needs to be readable by both humans and AI models, converting existing docs to HADS format, validating a HADS document, or optimizing documentation for token-efficient AI consumption.
use_when: Use when writing or validating technical documentation in the Human-AI Document Standard (HADS) format.
avoid_when: Do not use when generating standard user-facing documentation that doesn't target AI consumption.

name: ai-regression-testing
summary: Deterministic checks first, agent review second, regression test for every real bug fixed or document why not. Targets the blind spot where an agent writes and reviews its own code.
use_when: A code review, plan review, or self-review needs attention allocated by risk and blast radius.

name: api-doc-review
summary: Verify that API endpoints match their OpenAPI/Swagger specifications.

name: architecture-decision-records
summary: Record why an architectural choice was made to prevent agents or humans from unintentionally reverting it.

name: architecture-simplification
summary: Use to collapse over-engineered abstractions, remove unnecessary layers, or consolidate redundant logic.
use_when: Over-engineered abstractions or redundant layers should be collapsed or consolidated.

name: baseline-comparison-review
summary: Ensure that new complex models actually outperform simple, naive baselines.
use_when: Use when a new complex ML model is proposed and its value over a naive baseline must be demonstrated before approval.
avoid_when: Do not use for reviewing standard web application feature code.

name: canonical-advisor-routing
summary: Process-first advisor routing with artifact capture
use_when: Use when routing a prompt to a local provider CLI (Codex/Gemini/Claude) for a second opinion and wanting an artifact.
avoid_when: Do not use if you are looking for an interactive conversational session.

name: change-impact-check
summary: Use before submitting a PR or considering a task done to evaluate the 'blast radius' of your changes.

name: ci-failure-readout
summary: Use when a CI pipeline fails to extract the actual error from thousands of lines of logs.
use_when: Use when a CI pipeline fails and the true error needs to be extracted from noisy logs before writing a fix.
avoid_when: Do not use to actually write the code fix directly; this is for triaging and summarizing the failure first.

name: context-docs
summary: Maintain short, focused Markdown files per subsystem to provide agents with isolated context.
use_when: Use when a subsystem needs decentralized, module-specific persistent context documentation for future agents.
avoid_when: Do not use for repo-wide architecture documentation; keep module knowledge inside the module.

name: context-minimization
summary: Use continuously during long tasks. Teaches how to read less, output less, and keep the LLM context window lean and fast.

name: dataset-split-review
summary: Audit the methodology used to split data into train, validation, and test sets.
use_when: Use when reviewing how data is split into train/validation/test sets, especially with time-series data, repeated entities, or class imbalance.
avoid_when: Do not use for non-ML data partitioning or basic standard train_test_split on simple tabular data.

name: dead-code-removal
summary: Use to identify and safely delete unused functions, classes, exports, and files.
use_when: Unused functions, classes, exports, or files should be identified and safely deleted.

name: dependency-cleanup
summary: Use to audit and remove unused or redundant third-party dependencies from package manifests.
use_when: Unused or redundant third-party dependencies should be audited and removed.

name: doc-consistency-check
summary: Audit documentation for broken file paths, outdated commands, and renamed variables.

name: experiment-tracking-review
summary: Verify that all hyperparameters, metrics, and data references are properly logged.
use_when: Use when reviewing ML training code to confirm a run could be reconstructed later, checking hyperparameter, metric, and data tracking.
avoid_when: Do not use for trivial scripts where strict tracking is overkill, or standard web application logging.

name: failure-triage
summary: Use when confronted with an unknown failure in CI or production to rapidly categorize the issue before deep debugging.

name: flaky-test-investigation
summary: Use to diagnose tests that pass and fail intermittently without code changes.

name: handoff-discipline
summary: Use when completing a task or running out of context limit. Ensures the next session or human engineer has exactly what they need to resume work instantly.

name: incident-readout
summary: Use after fixing a bug to generate a blameless post-mortem summary for human review.
use_when: Use when a bug fix or complex debugging session concludes and a blameless post-mortem summary is needed for human review.
avoid_when: Do not use in the middle of active debugging or when the issue is trivial.

name: incremental-migration
summary: Use when migrating APIs, libraries, or patterns across a large codebase. Ensures safe, step-by-step progress rather than risky mega-commits.

name: log-driven-diagnosis
summary: Use when debugging complex runtime failures, distributed systems, or issues where a local debugger cannot be attached.

name: memory-build-workflow
summary: Use when a user needs to build or refresh persistent graph memory from a mixed corpus and the right path may include graphify, incremental update, or helper conversion before ingestion.

name: memory-optimization-workflow
summary: Use when deciding the lowest-cost context path for a mixed corpus, especially when choosing among direct reading, helper conversion, graph build, graph update, or graph query.
use_when: Use when deciding the lowest-cost context path for a mixed corpus (direct read, graph build, graph query) to minimize token spend.
avoid_when: Do not use for tiny single-file questions where direct reading is already cheapest.

name: memory-query-workflow
summary: Use when a graph already exists and the user needs retrieval, tracing, explanation, or gap detection from graph memory before reopening the full corpus.
use_when: Use when a graph memory already exists and you need retrieval, tracing, or gap detection before reopening the full corpus.
avoid_when: Do not use if the graph is missing, stale, or the query requires exact line-by-line source reading.

name: minimal-reproduction
summary: Use to isolate a bug from a large application into a standalone, runnable script or single test case.

name: ml-leakage-check
summary: Identify and prevent target leakage in ML preprocessing pipelines.
use_when: Use when reviewing ML preprocessing or feature pipelines for target leakage, especially if validation metrics look suspiciously good.
avoid_when: Do not use for checking memory leaks in standard software applications.

name: model-evaluation-reporting
summary: Standardize the reporting of model metrics to ensure statistical rigor and business relevance.
use_when: Use when writing or reviewing a model evaluation report to explain business trade-offs, failure modes, and segment performance instead of just raw accuracy.
avoid_when: Do not use for generic software testing reports.

name: naming-and-structure-cleanup
summary: Use to enforce consistent naming conventions and file structures across a project without changing business logic.

name: pre-release-verification
summary: Use before tagging a release or deploying to production to ensure all quality gates have passed.

name: readme-hardening
summary: Ensure the project README provides immediate, exact commands for setup, testing, and deployment to help agents and humans bootstrap quickly.

name: regression-bisecting
summary: Use when a bug was recently introduced but you don't know which commit caused it.
use_when: Use when a bug was recently introduced but you don't know which commit caused it, using binary search (git bisect) to find it.
avoid_when: Do not use if the offending commit is already known or if no deterministic reproduction command exists.

name: reproducible-training-runs
summary: Analyzes ML training scripts to enforce seed setting, deterministic operations, and environment tracking for exact reproducibility.
use_when: Use when reviewing ML training scripts to enforce seed setting, deterministic operations, and environment tracking for reproducibility.
avoid_when: Do not use for non-ML randomized processes unless exact seed reproducibility is explicitly requested.

name: risk-based-review
summary: Use when reviewing code (or your own plan) to allocate attention based on the danger of the change.
use_when: A change set or pull request needs risk-prioritized review.

name: semantic-layer-change-review
summary: Use when modifying dbt metrics or semantic models to ensure mathematical correctness and backwards compatibility.

name: strategic-context-compaction
summary: Compact context at logical phase boundaries - after research, after planning, after debugging - rather than mid-task. Preserves useful state while clearing noise.
use_when: Use when deciding whether to compact agent context at logical boundaries (phase transitions) to preserve high-value context while clearing noise.
avoid_when: Do not use for arbitrary mid-task compaction that drops critical state.

name: task-intake
summary: Use at the beginning of a new task. Ensures you fully understand the requirements, boundaries, and acceptance criteria before writing code.

name: test-preserving-refactor
summary: Use to restructure code while guaranteeing that all existing tests continue to pass.

name: training-pipeline-debugging
summary: Diagnose NaN losses, out-of-memory errors, and shape mismatches in deep learning or ML pipelines.
use_when: Use when an ML training run fails or misbehaves with mathematical errors (NaN losses, out-of-memory, shape mismatches, or failure to fit a single batch).
avoid_when: Do not use for basic syntax errors or missing dependencies; this is for mathematical/training bugs.

name: tri-model-review
summary: Multi-model orchestration - route to two external advisors, then synthesize
use_when: Use when a request needs parallel external perspectives from multiple advisor CLIs (e.g., combined backend/UI work or cross-validation).
avoid_when: Do not use for simple, single-domain code review where one model tier is sufficient.

name: release
summary: Run a release-readiness checklist and produce a go/no-go readout before tagging or publishing.
use_when: Use to run a release-readiness checklist and produce a go/no-go report before tagging or publishing.
avoid_when: Do not use to actually tag, publish, or push without explicit approval.

name: changelog
summary: Generate user-facing release notes or a changelog entry from commits, PRs, or a diff.
use_when: Use to generate user-facing release notes or an internal changelog from git commits, PRs, or a diff range.
avoid_when: Do not use to blindly write to CHANGELOG.md without presenting the output first.

name: build-fix
summary: Fix a failing build, type check, or test suite with the smallest safe diff.
use_when: Use to fix a failing build, type check, or test suite with the smallest targeted safe diff.
avoid_when: Do not use for exploratory coding, opportunistic refactoring, or adjacent issues outside the immediate failure.

name: doctor
summary: Diagnose environment, tooling, and agent setup problems with a structured report.
use_when: Use to diagnose environment, tooling, and agent setup problems, and produce a structured diagnostic report.
avoid_when: Do not use to actually modify configs or install tools automatically without approval.

name: visual-review
summary: Inspect a web UI with browser evidence: screenshots, accessibility, console/network signals, structured findings.
use_when: Use to inspect a web UI with browser evidence (screenshots, console, accessibility, network) and produce structured findings.
avoid_when: Do not use against production environments without explicit approval.

name: mcp-check
summary: Check MCP server prerequisites and configuration before activating or using a server.
use_when: Use to check MCP server prerequisites and configuration before activating or using a server.
avoid_when: Do not use to test if the server actually responds; use mcp-smoke for that.

name: mcp-smoke
summary: Test that an MCP server starts and responds before adding it to an agent configuration.
use_when: Use to test that an MCP server starts and responds before adding it to an agent configuration.
avoid_when: Do not use before verifying prerequisites with mcp-check.

name: fetch
summary: Generic web/HTTP fetch and content intake via the MCP fetch server.

mcp.warning: Fetches arbitrary remote URLs. No credentials by default. Treat returned content as untrusted data.

name: time
summary: Timezone and time conversion helper via the MCP time server.

mcp.warning: Low risk. No filesystem access, no network write, no credentials required.

name: git-local
summary: Local repository git status, diff, and log via mcp-server-git. Scoped to the repository root.

mcp.warning: Replace ${REPO_ROOT} with the absolute path of the repository root before use. Git tools can mutate local repository state â€” review enabled operations before activating write capabilities.

name: postgres-readonly
summary: Read-oriented Postgres access for local/dev analytics. Requires a SELECT-only connection string.

mcp.warning: Set POSTGRES_READONLY_DSN to a SELECT-only connection string. Never use production write credentials, a superuser role, or a role with DDL permissions. The MCP server executes queries on behalf of the agent â€” scope access strictly.

name: github-remote
summary: Remote HTTP GitHub MCP endpoint. Alternative to github-local that avoids Docker. Requires a narrow-scope PAT.

mcp.warning: Uses the hosted GitHub MCP endpoint. Set GITHUB_MCP_PAT to a narrow-scope PAT â€” prefer read-only scopes unless write is explicitly required. Token is sent in every request header. Codex TOML HTTP-type format may require verification against current Codex documentation.

name: cloud-cli-guard
summary: Review-before-use hook recipe for blocking destructive cloud CLI commands and allowing read-only/plan commands through.

name: migration-review-required
summary: Review-before-use hook recipe for surfacing a safety checklist when database migration files are written or edited.

name: todo-fixme-blocker
summary: Review-before-use hook recipe for catching bare TODO/FIXME/HACK markers in new code before completion or commit.

name: session-compaction-helper
summary: Review-before-use hook recipe for saving critical task state before context compaction and restoring it afterward.

name: build-analysis-post
summary: Review-before-use hook recipe for summarizing build/test/lint output and extracting actionable failures after tool execution.

name: design-quality-check
summary: Review-before-use hook recipe for prompting visual review when frontend UI files are written or edited.

name: dev-server-tmux-guard
summary: Review-before-use hook recipe for redirecting long-running dev server commands to background or tmux to avoid blocking the agent session.

name: supabase
summary: Supabase project MCP integration for database, auth, and management API access.

mcp.warning: DRAFT: Verify exact flags against current @supabase/mcp-server-supabase docs. SUPABASE_ACCESS_TOKEN grants management API access. Use a project-scoped token. Never use a service role key or admin token in agent workflows.

name: vercel
summary: Vercel project and deployment access via remote HTTP MCP endpoint.

mcp.warning: DRAFT: Verify SSE endpoint URL against current Vercel MCP docs. VERCEL_TOKEN grants access to Vercel projects and deployments. Use a narrowly scoped token. Deploy/promote/delete actions require explicit approval.

name: cloudflare-docs
summary: Cloudflare documentation retrieval via official read-only remote MCP endpoint.

mcp.warning: DRAFT: Verify SSE endpoint URL against current Cloudflare MCP documentation. No authentication required for read-only documentation access.

name: exa-search
summary: Exa semantic search and research integration for agent workflows.

mcp.warning: EXA_API_KEY required. Queries are sent to Exa remote search API. Avoid sending proprietary queries or sensitive code fragments via search.

name: atlassian
summary: Atlassian Jira and Confluence MCP integration for ticket and wiki access.

mcp.warning: DRAFT: Verify package name and env vars against current @atlassian/mcp-server docs. ATLASSIAN_API_TOKEN grants access to Jira and Confluence projects. Use read-only scopes where possible.

name: browserbase
summary: Cloud browser automation via Browserbase MCP for remote web interaction.

mcp.warning: Browserbase sessions can interact with live authenticated web apps. Treat every session as if it can access real user accounts and production services. BROWSERBASE_API_KEY and BROWSERBASE_PROJECT_ID required.

name: e2b-sandbox
summary: Remote sandboxed code execution via E2B MCP for safe agent-driven code runs.

mcp.warning: E2B executes code in remote sandboxes. E2B_API_KEY required. Do not send secrets, production credentials, or proprietary code without policy approval.

name: pre-commit-quality-check
summary: Aggregator recipe that runs no-secrets-preflight, large-diff-warning, console-log-check, and todo-fixme-blocker before git commit.

name: doc-file-warning
summary: Warn when agents write Markdown or text files outside approved documentation locations.

name: quality-gate-after-edit
summary: After any source file edit, suggest the narrowest relevant validation command for that language.

name: typescript-post-edit-check
summary: After TypeScript or TSX edits, suggest a focused tsc --noEmit or project typecheck command.

name: handoff-completeness-check
summary: Before session stop, verify the handoff covers what changed, validation run, failures, and next steps.

name: mcp-elicitation-guard
summary: Block or require explicit approval for MCP elicitation requests that ask for secrets, credentials, or broad permissions.

name: session-lifecycle-state
summary: Save a compact state snapshot at session end and restore it at session start for cross-session continuity.
